title: Hexagonal / Clean Architecture
summary: Ports & adapters pattern, dependency inversion at scale, keeping business logic free of framework concerns.

sections.content:
## The Core Idea

Hexagonal Architecture (Alistair Cockburn, 2005), also called Ports & Adapters, places the **domain model at the center** and isolates it from external concerns (databases, HTTP, messaging, UI) via ports (interfaces) and adapters (implementations).

The result: business logic has **zero dependency** on Spring, JPA, HTTP, or any infrastructure framework.

sections.title: Hexagonal Architecture
sections.content:
graph LR
    subgraph Adapters - Driving
        REST[REST Controller]
        CLI[CLI Adapter]
        MQ[Message Consumer]
    end
    subgraph Application Core
        AP[Application Port\nUseCase interface]
        Domain[Domain Model\nPure Java]
        DP[Domain Port\nRepository interface]
    end
    subgraph Adapters - Driven
        JPA[JPA Repository]
        Kafka[Kafka Producer]
        HTTP[HTTP Client]
    end
    REST -->|calls| AP
    CLI -->|calls| AP
    MQ -->|calls| AP
    AP --> Domain
    Domain -->|uses| DP
    DP <|-- JPA
    DP <|-- Kafka
    DP <|-- HTTP
    style Domain fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style AP fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style DP fill:#059669,color:#fff

sections.title: Dependency Direction
sections.content:
graph LR
    REST[REST Adapter] -->|depends on| Port[UseCase Port\ninterface]
    Port <|--implements| Service[OrderService\nDomain]
    Service -->|depends on| Repo[Repository Port\ninterface]
    Repo <|--implements| JPA[JPA Adapter]
    Note[Dependency always points INWARD\nCore never depends on infrastructure]
    style Service fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Port fill:#059669,color:#fff
    style Repo fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Project Structure

```
src/
  domain/
    model/
      Order.java          ← pure domain entity
      OrderLine.java
      Money.java          ← value object
    port/
      in/
        PlaceOrderUseCase.java     ← driving port (interface)
        GetOrderUseCase.java
      out/
        OrderRepository.java       ← driven port (interface)
        PaymentGateway.java
    service/
      OrderService.java   ← implements PlaceOrderUseCase, uses ports
  
  application/
    dto/
      PlaceOrderCommand.java
      OrderResponse.java
  
  infrastructure/
    web/
      OrderController.java       ← driving adapter
    persistence/
      JpaOrderRepository.java    ← driven adapter
      OrderJpaEntity.java        ← ORM mapping (not domain entity!)
    messaging/
      KafkaOrderPublisher.java   ← driven adapter
```

sections.question: In Hexagonal Architecture, where should the `OrderRepository` interface be defined?
options: In the infrastructure layer alongside the JPA implementation
In the domain/application core, as a driven port that the domain depends on
It should be auto-generated by Spring Data
sections.explanation: The OrderRepository interface (driven port) is defined in the domain/application core. This is the Dependency Inversion Principle — high-level modules (domain) should not depend on low-level modules (JPA). The domain defines what it needs via the interface; the infrastructure layer provides the implementation. This way the domain never imports JPA.
